one_line_takeaway: SIGMark replaces non-blind template-matching in-generation video watermarking with a global frame-wise PRC key scheme plus an optical-flow-based frame re-grouping module, achieving near-constant-time blind extraction and much stronger temporal-disturbance robustness than prior in-generation baselines, at a modest bit-accuracy cost relative to the non-blind VideoShield.
key_finding: On HunyuanVideo (Table 1), at 512-bit and 512x16=8192-bit watermark capacities, SIGMark (blind) achieves bit accuracy 0.958/0.885 (T2V) and 0.981/0.905 (I2V), versus VideoMark (non-blind) 0.873/0.758 (T2V) and 0.846/0.707 (I2V), and VideoShield (non-blind, requires original watermark info) 1.000/0.991 (T2V) and 1.000/0.999 (I2V); post-processing DCT/DT-CWT baselines reach comparable-ish bit accuracy (0.889/0.862, 0.619/0.650) but visibly degrade V-score (e.g., DCT V-score 0.424-0.456 vs no-mark 0.463-0.490) while in-generation methods leave V-score essentially unaffected. Under HunyuanVideo I2V robustness tests (Table 2), for temporal disturbances (drop/insert/clip of 30 frames) SIGMark degrades only 0.10/0.04/0.06 (0.91->0.81/0.87/0.85) versus VideoMark's much larger drops of 0.19-0.20 and VideoShield's 0.10-0.16 drops despite VideoShield being non-blind. Ablations (Table 3, HunyuanVideo I2V, 512x16-bit, temporal insert-30 disturbance) show GF-PRC raises bit acc from 0.707 (Single PRC, i.e., VideoMark-equivalent coding) to 0.905; SGO raises bit acc from 0.534 (w/o SGO, i.e., simple truncation) to 0.869, with OF-seg alone giving 0.762 and adding SW-det giving 0.823, i.e., both sub-components of SGO are needed. A scalability experiment (Figure 5, HunyuanVideo I2V, 512x16-bit, no disturbance) shows VideoShield's extraction time scales linearly with the number of generated videos on the platform while SIGMark's extraction time remains constant, since only the global PRC keys need to be stored/checked rather than per-request message-key pairs.
core_contribution: SIGMark is an in-generation (initial-noise) watermarking framework for video diffusion models that is the first to support BLIND extraction (no per-request message/key storage or template matching) while remaining robust to temporal disturbances on modern causal-3D-VAE video diffusion models. It has two mechanisms: (1) Global Frame-wise Pseudo-Random Coding (GF-PRC) -- a single global set of PRC keys (Christ & Gunn 2024 pseudorandom error-correcting codes), one key per causal latent-frame-group, shared across all generation requests, used to modulate the sign of Gaussian initial noise so watermark bits can be decoded directly from inverted noise without storing or matching against original messages; and (2) Segment Group-Ordering (SGO), a module that uses Farneback optical-flow segmentation plus a sliding-window PRC-based detector to recover the correct causal frame grouping/ordering of a temporally disturbed video (drop/insert/clip) before DDIM/flow-matching inversion, since causal 3D VAEs decode groups of dt adjacent frames from one latent temporal position and mis-grouping destroys invertibility.
threat_model: Not an adversarial/attacker-vs-defender threat model in the classical security-paper sense; the paper frames watermarking as an AI-safety/content-provenance mechanism (copyright/IP identification and tracing harmful/misleading AI-generated video). The 'adversary' modeled is generic content disturbance rather than an adaptive attacker: (a) spatial disturbances -- Gaussian noise, MPEG compression, blurring; (b) temporal disturbances -- random dropping, inserting, or clipping of 30 frames. No adaptive/white-box watermark-removal attacker, no evaluation against forgery of a fake message, and no cryptographic security proof of the PRC scheme's unforgeability is presented in the main text (a distortion-freeness proof is deferred to Appendix A, not reviewed here since only the first ~22 pages/main body were extracted).
limitations: The paper itself notes SIGMark does not attain 100% bit accuracy, attributed to the interplay between PRC's error-tolerance characteristics and diffusion-inversion accuracy across models (deferred analysis to Appendix E, not reviewed here). SIGMark trails the non-blind VideoShield baseline in raw bit accuracy in the no-disturbance setting (e.g., 0.958 vs 1.000, 0.885 vs 0.991 on HunyuanVideo T2V) -- the blind/scalable design trades some accuracy for constant-time extraction. The evaluation is limited to two open video diffusion models (HunyuanVideo, Wan-2.2) and to non-adaptive disturbances (noise, compression, blur, frame drop/insert/clip); there is no evaluation against an adaptive or white-box adversary attempting to remove/forge the watermark, no formal unforgeability/security analysis of the PRC-based scheme in the main text, and no human perceptibility study beyond the automated VBench-2.0 quality score. Only the extracted first ~22 pages (main body through references and start of appendix) were reviewed here, so Appendices A (distortion-free proof), B (extraction cost detail), C (optical-flow/sliding-window algorithm detail), D (Wan-2.2 results, prompt examples), and E (PRC robustness analysis) were not directly examined for this review.

datasets_used: VBench-2.0 (Zheng et al., 2025) — 400-video subset across 18 evaluation dimensions
quantitative_results: HunyuanVideo T2V bit accuracy: SIGMark 0.958 (512-bit) / 0.885 (512x16-bit) vs VideoMark 0.873/0.758 vs VideoShield 1.000/0.991 vs No-mark n/a
HunyuanVideo I2V bit accuracy: SIGMark 0.981/0.905 vs VideoMark 0.846/0.707 vs VideoShield 1.000/0.999
HunyuanVideo I2V V-score: SIGMark 0.506/0.499(T2V-table? see note)/0.472/0.488; no-mark baseline 0.490 (T2V) / 0.463 (I2V)

HunyuanVideo I2V robustness (Table 2, 512x16-bit): no-disturbance 0.91; frame drop 0.81 (-0.10); insert 0.87 (-0.04); clip 0.85 (-0.06); vs VideoMark no-disturbance 0.71, drop 0.52(-0.19), insert 0.51(-0.20), clip 0.51(-0.20); vs VideoShield no-disturbance 0.99, drop 0.89(-0.10), insert 0.84(-0.15), clip 0.83(-0.16)
HunyuanVideo I2V spatial robustness (512-bit): SIGMark w/o-disturbance 0.98, Gaussian noise 0.89(-0.09), compression 0.84(-0.14), blur 0.95(-0.03)
Ablation (Table 3, HunyuanVideo I2V, 512x16-bit under 30-frame insert): Single PRC 0.707 -> GF-PRC 0.905; w/o SGO 0.534 -> w/o OF-seg 0.762 -> w/o SW-det 0.823 -> full SGO 0.869
Scalability: extraction time of non-blind VideoShield scales linearly with number of generated videos; SIGMark's extraction time is constant (Figure 5, qualitative, no exact numbers extracted)
